# browsegrab

> Token-efficient browser agent for local LLMs — Playwright + accessibility tree + MarkGrab, MCP native.

## Purpose

browsegrab provides programmatic browser automation optimized for low-token-budget LLM agents. It renders pages via Playwright and exposes the accessibility tree (~500-1,500 tokens per step) instead of raw HTML, with optional MarkGrab integration for clean markdown extraction. Elements are referenced by short IDs (e1, e2, ...) for compact action commands.

## Capabilities

- **Accessibility tree snapshots**: Navigate to a URL and get a token-efficient tree representation of the page
- **Element interaction**: Click, type, scroll using short ref IDs from the accessibility tree
- **Content extraction**: Extract page content as compressed DOM (AX tree + markdown via MarkGrab)
- **Agentic browsing**: LLM-driven multi-step browsing toward an objective with action planning
- **MCP server**: 8 browser automation tools exposed via FastMCP (stdio transport)

## Installation

```
pip install browsegrab          # core (Playwright + httpx)
pip install browsegrab[cli]     # CLI (click + rich)
pip install browsegrab[mcp]     # MCP server (fastmcp)
pip install browsegrab[content] # content extraction (markgrab)
pip install browsegrab[all]     # everything
```

## Quick Use

### CLI

```bash
browsegrab snapshot https://example.com          # accessibility tree snapshot
browsegrab extract https://example.com           # extract content as markdown
browsegrab browse https://example.com "find the pricing page"  # agentic browse
```

### Python

```python
from browsegrab import BrowseGrabConfig, BrowseSession

async with BrowseSession(BrowseGrabConfig.from_env()) as session:
    await session.navigate("https://example.com")
    snap = await session.snapshot()          # SnapshotResult
    content = await session.extract_content() # markdown string
    result = await session.browse("https://example.com", "find pricing")  # BrowseResult
```

### MCP

Server: `browsegrab-mcp` (stdio transport)
Tools: `browser_navigate`, `browser_click`, `browser_type`, `browser_snapshot`, `browser_scroll`, `browser_extract_content`, `browser_go_back`, `browser_wait`

## Links

- Docs: https://github.com/QuartzUnit/browsegrab
- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/browsegrab/
- Source: https://github.com/QuartzUnit/browsegrab
